responseText:
Absolutely — I pulled your **Rivian R1S (ToughNut)** data for the full **10-day window: Feb 27 → Mar 8, 2026 (inclusive)**.

## 10-day activity summary (real TezLab data)

- **Total driving:** **1,077.9 mi** across **74 drives**
- **Total charging:** **669.7 kWh** across **17 sessions**
- **Charging cost:** **$54.10**
- **Estimated savings:** **$42.76**
- **Idle loss:** **124.3 mi** of range

And yes — there is clear activity in **February**:
- **Feb 27:** 118.4 mi, 2 charges (17.2 kWh)
- **Feb 28:** 70.8 mi, 3 charges (122.7 kWh)

## Daily breakdown (all 10 days)

| Date | Drive mi | Drives | Charge kWh | Charges |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2026-02-27 | 118.4 | 10 | 17.2 | 2 |
| 2026-02-28 | 70.8 | 4 | 122.7 | 3 |
| 2026-03-01 | 131.1 | 5 | 0.0 | 0 |

| 2026-03-04 | 206.0 | 5 | 108.2 | 2 |
| 2026-03-05 | 96.2 | 10 | 118.2 | 2 |

| 2026-03-07 | 10.0 | 4 | 14.5 | 1 |
| 2026-03-08 | 60.2 | 6 | 0.0 | 0 |

## Notable trips (longest drives in this period)

1. **123.5 mi** — Ludlow, VT → Canaan, CT (Mar 1)  
2. **114.5 mi** — Lebanon, NH → Holyoke, MA (Mar 4)  
3. **93.6 mi** — Cornwall, CT → Bennington, VT (Feb 27)  
4. **77.2 mi** — West Lebanon, NH → Stowe, VT (Mar 3)  
5. **71.9 mi** — Great Barrington, MA → Dover, VT (Mar 2)

## Narrative of the 10-day window

This looks like a **high-mobility regional loop through VT/NH/MA/CT**, with heavier movement in the middle of the period:

- **Feb 27–28:** You kicked off with meaningful mileage and multiple charges, including a long Cornwall→Bennington leg and follow-on movement to Ludlow.
- **Mar 1–4:** This was the busiest stretch. You stacked several long intercity drives (Ludlow→Canaan, then NH/VT/MA corridors), with **Mar 4 as the peak driving day (206 mi)**.
- **Charging strategy:** A mix of **overnight AC sessions** (large kWh adds in Cornwall/Dover/Ludlow) and **high-power DC fast stops** (e.g., Lebanon/Holyoke/West Lebanon, ~215–218 kW max).
- **Mar 5–8:** Activity tapered from travel-heavy to shorter/local driving, bottoming on Mar 7 (10 mi), then rebounding to 60.2 mi on Mar 8.

If you want, I can next turn this into:
- a **trip-purpose hypothesis** (commute vs multi-stop travel vs weekend circuit),
- or a **day-by-day map-style itinerary summary** with the major route arcs.
